# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1beta2   # Use the correct version based on your kubeconfig context configuration  (e.g., v1, beta3)
kind: Deployment         # Kind of resource we are creating in Kubernetes; it can be a Pod or any other API object here as per requirement mentioned above e.g deployment for stateful application and daemonSet etc.   .Kind should match the type specified by your kind configuration  (e.g., ReplicaSets, StatefulSets).
metadata:                # Metadata attached to our resource - Name is mandatory in Kubernetes otherwise it's considered as invalid object also metadata will hold name and labels fields of an API Object    .Name should match the display-name set using 'kubectl annotate --overwrite'.  (e.g., myFirstResource).
   name: logging_agent     # Name/display-name for our resource to identify it in k8s resources command or list view and describe output etc.(must be unique within a namespace, optional when not specified - defaults as 'default').    .Name should match the display-name set using "kubectl annotate --overwrite".  (e.g., myFirstResource).
   annotations:           # Optional fields that contain extra information about our resource e.g label or taints, for more info on these you may refer to https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api_conventions.md#metadata
     application: logging  # This is used by controllers (like StatefulSet) that need information about the created objects in order to form a new Deployment, for more details check out related resources https://github.com/kubernetes/community/.   .application should match with your project name e.g., myFirstResourceApp
     version: v1         # This field can be used by controller of such as liveness probe in k8s objects to know the type and current state for created object (for example, if we are updating a existing deployment then this would provide info about updated resource).   .version should match with your project name e.g., myFirstResourceAppVersion
     component: logging-agent  # This is used by controllers(like StatefulSet) to form the identifier for object and in case of DaemonSets it helps uniquely identify a pod that has been assigned this controller’s daemonset role, based on its name.   .component should match with your project's component e.g., myFirstResourceAppComponent
spec:                    # Configuration specification (replace fields as per requirement)  This is the resource configuration for Deployment object in kubernetes if it contains replicas and template/pod definition etc..Replicas refers how many pod instances to create - minimum of one usually set according to your needs.Template specifies what resources will be created when a new Pod instance is started,such as which image(s) should use for the newly instantiated container within each Deployment’Sandbox and any command that gets run in order if firstPodCreation=true – eithe an Image or Command array can hold only one option.
   replicas: 1            # Number of pod instances to maintain alive (defaults to none, meaning it is set according by your needs).    .replicas should match with number you desire the logging agent has running in a cluster etc., must be integer and more than zero if specified otherwise will default as one.
   selector:              # Logically identifies which pods instance(instances) are going to run (pod names), can't select nothing eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required for ReplicaSet, PodAntiAffinity and related fields if set then must contain labels.
     matchLabels:          # Specifies which pod(instances) should be selected to run as per selector (label keys).   .matchLabelKeys can't select nothing eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required for PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity etc related fields if set then must contain labels.
       application: logging    # Label key to uniquely identify pods created by DaemonSets in k8s (for instance the name we provided above).   .application should match with your project's label e.g., myFirstResourceAppLoggingAgentLabelKey  and same for version, component etc as well according you app requirement
       type: logging-agent    # Label key to uniquely identify pod(instances) that have been assigned the role of daemonset in k8s (for instance our name we provided above).   .type should match with your project's label e.g., myFirstResourceAppLoggingAgentLabelType
     template:             # Specifies what resources will be created when a new Pod is started and for how long until the termination of old pod(instances) – minPodRunningSeconds, maxSurge must specify to set in seconds as well minimum allowed time between terminating one running container.eithe they can't both exist if either has been specified then all are mandatory fields).
       metadata:            # Metadata attached to the Pod(instances) created – this is for specifying a configuration file, environment variables etc which should be passed when starting pods from its template (spec/containers[0]), read more here - https://kubernetes.io/.   .metadata can't select nothing eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required for PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity etc related fields if set then must contain labels in this case metadata
         annotations:        # Optional Fields that hold additional information about the resource created as per requirement. (for instance configMapKeyRef and volumeMount references) read more here - https://kubernetes.io/.   .annotations can't select nothing eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required for PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity etc related fields if set then must contain labels in this case annotations
           configMapKeyRef:    # Configmap key reference from which to take the values.read more here - https://kubernetes.io/.   .configmaps can't select nothing eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required for PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity etc related fields if set then must contain labels in this case configmapKeyRefs
             name: logging_agent    # Name/displayName provided as configuration file / environment variable reference (for instance myLoggingAgentConfigMap).   .name should match with your project's named e.g., myFirstResourceApploggingAgentsetupconfigurations and same for volumeMount etc according to requirement
           volumes:            # Volume definitions that provide values in the pod(instances), can be used as a reference (eithe it needs all containers or none of them – only required if spec/containers[0]'s Volumes is specified, which could have its definition) read more here - https://kubernetes.io/.   .volumes and specify the volume mount points in order to provide values for a given pod(instance).
             configMap:            # Configmap definitions that hold environment variables or configuration files data (can be used as reference if spec/containers[0]'s Volumes is specified, which could have its definition) read more here - https://kubernetes.io/.   .configMaps can’t select nothing eithe it needs all containers but none of them – only required for PodDisruptionBudget related fields if set then must contain labels in this case configmap
               name: logging_agent    # Name/displayName provided as configuration file / environment variable reference (for instance myLoggingAgentConfigMap).   .name should match with your project's named e.g.,  and same for volumeMount etc according to requirement`        ], [...] }], which are used by Kubernetes API object if we need additional Pod(Instances) configured, like in this case of multiple logs collection pod as well setting a correct path or port binding between logging agent container within the deployment.
